meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Fix Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how a SaaS starter kit and AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code can help you ship faster and debug efficiently.
content.answer_block: Stop manually patching broken code. By utilizing a production-ready SaaS starter kit, you inherit battle-tested architecture that eliminates common debugging pitfalls. Combine this with AI-driven analysis from Antigravity and Claude Code to automate your error handling, reclaim your development velocity, and deliver flawless client projects in half the time.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your sprint, your client is asking for an update, and your terminal is bleeding red error logs. You are stuck in the 'debugging death loop,' where every fix creates two new bugs. It’s the silent killer of freelance careers, turning high-margin projects into bottomless pits of unpaid maintenance hours.

You don’t need to be a better debugger; you need a better foundation. By shifting to a standardized SaaS starter kit, you stop reinventing the wheel and start building on a bedrock of proven code. Combined with the surgical precision of AI-assisted refactoring, you can transition from a stressed code-wrangler to a high-velocity product architect.

steps.title: Standardize Your Boilerplate
steps.description: Initialize a project using a vetted SaaS starter kit to ensure your authentication, database schemas, and API routes are pre-configured, reducing the surface area for bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and compare it against industry-standard SaaS starter kits. Identify structural weaknesses that lead to frequent debugging bottlenecks.

steps.title: Automated Error Surface Mapping
steps.description: Use AI to map your current error logs to specific modules within your starter kit, identifying if the bug is a custom implementation error or a systemic architectural flaw.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan the logs in /logs/error.log. Categorize these errors by module and determine if they stem from my custom code or the boilerplate core.

steps.title: Contextual Refactoring
steps.description: Leverage your starter kit’s design patterns to rewrite buggy functions, ensuring they adhere to the established project standards rather than writing quick, fragile patches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the authentication middleware in /lib/auth.ts to match the modular pattern used in the starter kit's documentation, ensuring type safety and error handling.

steps.title: AI-Driven Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Prevent regressions by generating comprehensive unit tests for your newly refactored modules, ensuring the starter kit's integrity remains intact.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest unit tests for the core logic in /services/payment.ts, covering edge cases identified in the recent debugging session.

steps.title: Continuous Validation Loop
steps.description: Implement a CLI-based oversight loop where AI checks every PR against the starter kit's architectural constraints before deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a validation script that runs before git commit, checking all new code against the architectural rules defined in the starter kit's README.

pro_tips: Always wrap third-party API calls in a standardized 'Adapter' pattern provided by your starter kit to isolate debugging to one file.
Use Claude Code to document your custom overrides within the starter kit to ensure future maintenance doesn't break your core logic.
Monitor 'Ghost Errors' in your starter kit by setting up automated heartbeat logs that ping your dashboard every 5 minutes.
If you find yourself debugging the same module twice, move that logic into a shared utility library within the kit.
Leverage the 'Strict Mode' settings in your starter kit's TypeScript config to catch null-pointer exceptions before they reach runtime.
content.next_steps: Once your codebase is stabilized, focus on automating your deployment pipeline to ensure your starter kit remains updated with the latest security patches.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with fragile code and start shipping products that scale. By mastering your starter kit and AI-assisted workflows, you'll reduce your debugging time by 70% and impress every client you work with. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
